phase3_findings.description: Stale hardcoded 'vs main' in the review skill's Input line (:11) and reviewer-prompt Description template (:1033) — the exact hardcoded-base class this PR set out to eliminate; misdescribes behavior for non-main-trunk consumers.

The $PR_BASE_BRANCH operand producer (Phase 0.2 'gh pr view --json …baseRefName…' field + storage instruction) is entirely unpinned; a future edit dropping baseRefName silently empties the operand every base-resolution arm reads while the suite stays green (CLAUDE.md operand→producer gotcha).

review-and-fix item 6a prose claims the config-get hard-failure fallback to 'main' is 'the same shape … update-branch-checkpoint.sh use[s]', but the checkpoint script emits UNVERIFIED (stops) on hard failure rather than defaulting — fail-open-to-a-guess mislabeled 'fail-closed'.

The retargeting divergence warning reads $BASE from a separate fence; if the agent does not thread $BASE, it is empty here and the warning fires on every head-override run (over-warns, fails safe — log noise only).
item-6a current-branch arm is unreachable in the PR-scoped fix loop (PR_BASE_BRANCH always set); retained only for byte-parity with Phase 0.2 but not annotated as such.

fix_decisions.claim_text: divergence warning reads $BASE from a separate fence — over-warns if not threaded
fix_decisions.evidence: Fails safe (over-warns, log noise only, never leaks or mis-approves). The obvious fix (re-resolve $BASE inside the HEAD_OVERRIDE_BASE_RESOLUTION fence) would add a second 'config-get.sh .base_branch main' occurrence to review/SKILL.md, flipping the #503 AC8 pin (expects exactly 1) RED; the prose already instructs the agent to thread the resolved value like $PR_BASE_BRANCH/$PR_BASE_SHA. Parked as advisory.

fix_decisions.claim_text: item-6a current-branch arm unreachable in the fix loop, retained for Phase-0.2 byte-parity but not annotated
fix_decisions.evidence: Cosmetic doc clarification; the arm is intentionally byte-identical to Phase 0.2 (AC8 extracts and compares the fence between BEGIN/END markers), so adding an in-fence note risks the parity assertion. Low value. Parked as advisory.

rows: UNRESOLVABLE	R3	.changeset/issue-503-diff-base-fix.md	6	count-locked: '3 item' claim but no adjacent assertion block (informational, never gates)

skipped_checks.reason: working tree dirty at the pre-commit suite run (this check grades committed HEAD); re-run on the clean committed tree passed with 0 skipped (7688 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped)

phase3_findings.description: N1: 'Both local-diff producers check the raw diff, filtered candidate, promotion write, and stdout publication' is false for the item-6a pre-check (only Phase 0.2 has that pipeline). Appears in .changeset/issue-503-diff-base-fix.md:6 and docs/DEVFLOW_SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md:371.

N2: item-6a PR-mode branch (exit 65 guard + REVIEW_DIFF_BASE=$HEAD_OVERRIDE_BASE) is never executed and pinned deletion-only; guard inversion or exit-65→exit-0 stays green. Corroborated by silent-failure-hunter + requesting-code-review.
run.sh assertion label says 'stages the filtered diff' but pins the raw-candidate (unfiltered) stage.
head-override prose line 'git diff origin/$PR_BASE_BRANCH...HEAD' not pinned against reverting to the stale SHA; Scenario-B fixture not run end-to-end through the fence.
